问题导向句式: However, obtaining the shape of glossy objects like metals, plastics or ceramics remains challenging, since standard Lambertian cues like photo-consistency cannot be easily applied.
Inferring the depth of transparent or mirror (ToM) surfaces represents a hard challenge for either sensors, algorithms, or deep networks.
对比转折句式: However, most current depth estimation methods support only Lambertian scenes, making them unreliable for glossy surfaces.
On the one hand, the definition of depth itself might appear ambiguous in such cases... On the other hand, as humans can deal with this through experience...
方法提出句式: In this paper, we derive a spatially-varying (SV)BRDF-invariant theory for recovering 3D shape and reflectance from light-field cameras.
In this paper, we propose a novel decoupling-and-aggregating strategy and develop a dual-layer depth estimation network for LF images with complex reflections.
因果推导句式: This difficulty arises because ToM surfaces introduce misleading visual information about scene geometry, which makes depth estimation challenging...
Such situations break Lambertian assumptions, leading to the wrong depth estimation results of most existing depth estimation algorithms.

段落组织.段落开头引入方式: 通常以宏观背景陈述、日常现象引入，或直接概述当前技术领域的主流方法及现状来开篇（如'Light-fields implicitly capture...', 'In our daily lives, we often interact with...', 'Many depth estimation methods... have been proposed.'）。
段落组织.段内论证逻辑: 多采用'漏斗型'演绎逻辑，即'陈述背景/现状 -> 使用转折词（However/Unfortunately）指出当前方法的局限性或未解决的挑战 -> 剖析导致该问题的根本原因 -> 顺理成章地引出本文的解决思路或具体模块'。
段落组织.段落结尾收束方式: 常以总结本段核心贡献、列举具体创新点（如'Our main contributions are: 1)...'）、强调本文方法相较于前人工作的独特优势（如'...which has not been achieved by previous...'），或通过承上启下的过渡句引出下一章节的具体实现细节来收尾。

用词特征: 过渡词：However, Moreover, Therefore, In contrast, Specifically, Unfortunately, In short, Purposely, Additionally, On the one hand / On the other hand.
强调词：explicitly, dramatically, remarkably, extensively, accurately, significantly, sufficiently, solely.
限定词：primarily, mostly, generally, locally, spatially-varying, under-constrained, real-world, state-of-the-art, dual-layer.
学术动词与短语：derive, propose, demonstrate, validate, formulate, address, tackle, leverage, exploit, violate, break assumptions, fine-tune, in-painting.
论证模式: 提出问题→分析原因→给出方案 (Problem-Cause-Solution)：首先指出透明/反光表面深度估计的困难（Problem），接着深入分析是因为违反了Lambertian假设、代价体积出现混叠双峰分布或引入了误导性视觉信息（Cause），最后提出解耦聚合网络、伪标签微调或SVBRDF不变理论等针对性方案（Solution）。

先总后分/总分总 (General-to-Specific)：在引言或方法概述部分先总体提出本文的核心框架（如decoupling-and-aggregating strategy），随后分段详细拆解并论述各个子模块（如adaptive cost volume decoupling module, iterative self-guided aggregating module）的具体作用与实现机制。
批判性对比论证 (Critical Comparative Argumentation)：在相关工作或引言中，先列举并肯定现有经典方法，随后通过转折指出其在特定场景（如非Lambertian表面）下的失效原因，最后对比凸显本文方法在鲁棒性、适用范围或精度上的优越性。

引用风格.引用的嵌入方式: 以句末引用（End-of-sentence）最为常见，用于支撑前置陈述（如'...remains a challenging problem... [64]'）；其次为句中/主语引用（Mid-sentence），将作者或具体方法作为句子主语（如'Tao et al. [27] proposed...'）；偶尔使用括号内补充引用，用于举例或补充说明（如'(e.g. Lytro [1] and Raytrix [22])'）。
引用风格.引用与论述的衔接方式: 1. 归纳式衔接：将多篇文献归为一类进行总体评述（如'Many depth estimation methods... have been proposed [9, 13, 15...]'）；2. 批判式衔接：引用具体文献后紧跟转折词指出其局限性（如'Tao et al. [27] proposed... However, they attempt...'）；3. 顺承/奠基式衔接：将引用作为本文理论的基石或灵感来源（如'...follow the shape estimation framework... proposed by Chandraker [5, 6, 7]'）。

章节结构.子节名称: 研究背景与动机 (Background & Motivation)
章节结构.核心任务: 引入研究领域的宏观背景，阐述该研究在实际应用、工业需求或理论层面的重要性与迫切性，确立研究的合法性与价值。

章节结构.子节名称: 问题陈述与现有局限 (Problem Statement & Limitations)
章节结构.核心任务: 聚焦具体研究问题，深度剖析现有方法、技术或传感器的固有缺陷、假设限制及面临的挑战，明确指出当前领域的Research Gap。

章节结构.子节名称: 本文方法与核心思想 (Proposed Method & Core Idea)
章节结构.核心任务: 针对上述局限，顺势引出本文提出的解决方案，概述核心算法、策略、技术路线或关键假设，建立从问题到方案的桥梁。

章节结构.子节名称: 主要贡献总结 (Main Contributions)
章节结构.核心任务: 以结构化的列表形式，明确、精炼地总结本文在理论推导、算法设计、数据集构建或实验验证等方面的具体、可量化的贡献。

Introduction.内容逻辑: 整体呈现“漏斗式（Funnel-shaped）”递进逻辑：从“宏观背景（Why it matters）”收敛至“具体痛点（What is wrong）”，再反转至“本文方案（How we fix it）”，最后落脚于“具体成果（What we achieved）”。过渡方式上：1. 背景到局限使用强转折词（如However, Unfortunately, Despite）打破理想状态，引入现实困境；2. 局限到方法使用总结或目的导向词（如In this paper, Purposely, In short）实现从“破”到“立”的转换；3. 方法到贡献使用引导句（如Our main contributions are）将抽象方法具象化为条目化的成果。
论证手法: 数据支撑：使用权威统计数据量化问题的严重性或普遍性（如引用建筑能耗占比、室内停留时间比例等数据）。
对比论证：横向对比不同技术路线的优劣（如对比侵入式、半侵入式与非侵入式热舒适测量方法的局限性），凸显本文方法的优越性。
举例说明：列举生活或工业中的具体场景与实体（如建筑玻璃窗、汽车反光面、智能手环），增强问题的直观性和读者代入感。
文献佐证与批判：引用前人工作确立研究基线，并精准指出其假设失效的边界条件（如指出二向色性模型对金属材料失效）。
图表直观论证：在引言中直接引用核心图表（如Fig. 1），通过视觉对比（如原模型与微调后模型的深度图预测对比）直观证明方法的有效性。

研究背景与动机.核心句式: In [context], [phenomenon/problem] is/are [adjective] challenges. / [Subject] constitutes one of the primary sources of [impact].
研究背景与动机.原文示例: In modern society, global climate change and energy scarcity are increasingly acute challenges. Building energy consumption constitutes one of the primary sources of global energy expenditure...

问题陈述与现有局限.核心句式: However, most current [methods] support only [assumption], making them unreliable for [target]. / Unfortunately, [factor] violate the working principles of [technology].
问题陈述与现有局限.原文示例: However, most current depth estimation methods support only Lambertian scenes, making them unreliable for glossy surfaces. / Unfortunately, light reflection and refraction over ToM surfaces violate also the working principles of most active depth sensors...

本文方法与核心思想.核心句式: In this paper, we present a [method] that explicitly models/handles [feature]. / Purposely, this paper proposes a [adjective] strategy for [objective].
本文方法与核心思想.原文示例: In this paper, we present a depth and reflectance estimation algorithm that explicitly models spatially varying BRDFs (SVBRDFs) from light-field cameras. / Purposely, this paper proposes a simple yet effective strategy for obtaining training data and, thereby, dramatically boosting the accuracy...

主要贡献总结.核心句式: Our main contributions are: 1) A [noun] of [concept]... 2) A [method] algorithm that handles...
主要贡献总结.原文示例: Our main contributions are: 1) A generalization of optical flow to the non-Lambertian case in light-field cameras. 2) A depth estimation algorithm for light-field cameras that handles diffuse plus specular 1-lobe BRDFs.

章节结构.子节名称: Depth from Light-Field Cameras
章节结构.核心任务: 回顾光场相机深度估计的现有方法，指出其在处理非朗伯体（高光/光泽表面）时的局限性，并引出本文方法在通用BRDF和反射率恢复上的优势。

章节结构.子节名称: Shape from Shading
章节结构.核心任务: 回顾阴影恢复形状的经典与近期方法，指出其通常依赖已知光源或朗伯体假设，说明本文问题设定更具挑战性，并说明对局部多项式优化策略的借鉴。

章节结构.子节名称: Differential Motion Theory
章节结构.核心任务: 回顾微分运动理论这一核心理论基础，详细对比本文方法与该理论在相机运动类型、视角变化和BRDF不变性方程推导上的核心差异与理论创新。

Related Work.内容逻辑: 该章节采用了“应用领域现状 -> 相关经典问题 -> 核心理论基础”的递进逻辑。首先从最直接的应用场景（光场深度估计）出发，指出当前技术在非朗伯体表面的瓶颈；接着拓展到相关的传统视觉问题（阴影恢复形状），分析其物理假设的局限性并借鉴其优化策略；最后深入到本文所依赖的底层数学与物理理论（微分运动理论），通过详细的条件对比，论证现有理论在纯平移光场设定下的不适用性，从而自然引出本文的理论创新。各子节之间通过“指出局限-提出本文改进”的Gap Analysis（研究空白分析）模式进行平滑过渡。
论证手法: 归纳与分类：将现有方法按假设条件进行分类（如Lambertian与specular的二分类，或依赖已知光源的方法），梳理研究脉络。
缺陷分析（Gap Analysis）：深入剖析前人方法在特定物理条件下的失效原因（如dichromatic model对金属失效，line consistency对相似颜色不鲁棒）。
对比论证（Contrast）：高频使用对比手法，将前人方法的局限（如包含旋转运动、假设恒定视角）与本文方法的设定（纯平移、考虑视角变化）进行直接对比，凸显本文的创新性。
理论/几何条件推导：通过分析具体的几何或物理条件（如法线必须位于光源方向的圆锥上、视角方向不差异意味着什么）来严谨证明现有理论在本文设定下不成立。

Depth from Light-Field Cameras.介绍现状与局限: Many [methods] have been proposed. However, most of them rely on the [assumption] and work poorly on [specific cases]. (原文: Many depth estimation methods for light-field cameras have been proposed. However, most of them rely on the Lambertian assumption and work poorly on glossy surfaces.)
Depth from Light-Field Cameras.指出近期工作不足: [Author] proposed a [method] that [action]. However, they attempt [limitation], which cannot handle [general cases]. (原文: Tao et al. proposed a clustering method that eliminates specular pixels... However, they attempt a binary classification... which cannot handle general glossy surfaces.)
Depth from Light-Field Cameras.引出本文优势: In contrast, our model can work on [general condition], and can also [additional capability], which has not been achieved by previous [domain] approaches. (原文: In contrast, our model can work on general 1-lobe BRDFs, and can also recover reflectance in addition to shape... which has not been achieved by previous light-field shape acquisition approaches.)

Shape from Shading.介绍经典问题与假设: Since it is a very under-constrained problem, most work assumes [condition] to increase feasibility. (原文: Since it is a very under-constrained problem, most work assumes a known light source to increase feasibility.)
Shape from Shading.借鉴策略并指出差异: We adopt this strategy in our [procedure]. However, note that our case is harder, since most [methods] are limited to [assumption]. (原文: We adopt this strategy in our final optimization procedure. However, note that our case is harder, since most shape from shading methods are limited to Lambertian surfaces.)

Differential Motion Theory.指出最相关理论: Our theoretical contributions are most closely related to the [theory] proposed by [Author]. (原文: Our theoretical contributions are most closely related to the differential theory proposed by Chandraker.)
Differential Motion Theory.详细对比差异: Our work has [number] major differences. First, in contrast to [previous method], we [our method]. Second, the work by [Author] assumes [assumption]. In contrast, for our [setup], we must [our requirement]. (原文: Our work has three major differences. First, in contrast to the differential motions he uses... we only have translations... Second, the work by Chandraker then assumes a constant viewing direction... In contrast, for our purely translational light-field setup, we must account for viewpoint variations.)

章节结构.子节名称: Methodology Overview (方法概述)
章节结构.核心任务: 总体介绍提出的框架（如TLA-DETR），明确研究目标（解决红外数据稀缺下的检测问题），并概述核心方法论（两阶段跨域迁移学习）。

章节结构.子节名称: Stage 1: Pre-training on Visible-light Datasets (第一阶段：可见光数据集预训练)
章节结构.核心任务: 详细说明在源域（大规模可见光数据集）上训练特征提取和检测架构的过程、网络结构细节及预训练目标。

章节结构.子节名称: Stage 2: Cross-domain Transfer Learning Strategy (第二阶段：跨域迁移学习策略)
章节结构.核心任务: 深入解析从可见光域到红外域的迁移机制，重点阐述独特的迁移技巧（冻结CNN骨干网络、Transformer编码器/解码器参数共享、仅微调前馈神经网络FFNs）及其在few-shot条件下的作用。

章节结构.子节名称: Loss Function and Optimization (损失函数与优化细节)
章节结构.核心任务: 定义模型训练所使用的损失函数（如分类损失、边界框回归损失等），并说明优化器设置和训练超参数。

Methodology.内容逻辑: 整体采用“总-分”与“时间/流程顺序”相结合的逻辑。首先通过Overview进行全局统筹，点明“两阶段”核心思想；随后按照算法的实际执行流程，先论述第一阶段的源域预训练（奠定特征提取基础），再论述第二阶段的跨域迁移（解决目标域数据稀缺的核心痛点），最后补充损失函数等训练细节。各子节之间通过数据流向和模型状态的演进（从预训练权重到迁移微调）进行自然过渡，逻辑链条严密。
论证手法: 机制解释：通过详细解释“冻结CNN+参数共享+微调FFN”的具体操作，论证该策略如何有效防止在few-shot条件下过拟合，从而实现高效域适应。
流程描述：按照两阶段框架的执行步骤，清晰描述模型从源域到目标域的学习过程，证明方法的可行性。
对比论证（隐含）：在提出“unique transfer learning trick”时，隐含了与全参数微调或传统微调方法的对比，突出所提策略在红外数据稀缺场景下的优越性。
数学推导（常规补充）：在损失函数和网络结构部分，使用严谨的数学公式对前向传播和误差计算进行定义，增强方法的科学性和可复现性。

关键句式模板.方法概述: [Model Name] employs a [framework type] framework, aiming to address the challenge of [specific task] under [specific condition/data scarcity]. (原文示例: TLA-DETR employs a two-stage cross-domain transfer learning framework, aiming to address the challenge of efficient detection and location of indoor thermal adaptation behaviors under infrared data scarcity.)
关键句式模板.核心原理: Its core principle involves [action 1] on [source domain/dataset], then [action 2] to the [target domain] through [specific technique/trick] to achieve [final goal]. (原文示例: Its core principle involves pre-training a robust feature extraction and detection architecture on large-scale visible-light datasets, then transferring visual information to the infrared domain through a unique transfer learning trick to achieve efficient domain adaptation.)
关键句式模板.具体策略展开: ...through a unique [strategy name] ([technique 1] + [technique 2] + [technique 3]). (原文示例: ...through a unique transfer learning trick (freezing the CNN backbone + parameter sharing of the Transformer encoder/decoder + fine-tuning only the FFNs).)
关键句式模板.阶段过渡: In the first/second stage, the model is [action] on [dataset/domain] to learn/transfer [feature/knowledge].

章节结构.子节名称: 实验设置与总体评估 (Experimental Setup & Overall Evaluation)
章节结构.核心任务: 介绍所使用的数据集（如HCInew, Inria等）、评估指标，并概述方法在标准基准上的整体性能提升和代码开源情况。

章节结构.子节名称: 与SOTA方法的对比实验 (Comparison with State-of-the-Art)
章节结构.核心任务: 在公开数据集上与现有最先进方法进行全面的定量（表格指标如MSE, Bad Pix）和定性（视觉深度图、误差图）比较，确立方法的总体优越性。

章节结构.子节名称: 敏感性与鲁棒性分析 (Sensitivity and Robustness Analysis)
章节结构.核心任务: 通过控制变量实验，探究模型对特定物理属性或干扰因素（如透明度、反射率、噪声、视差范围、纹理尺度）的敏感度和性能极限。

章节结构.子节名称: 消融实验与参数分析 (Ablation Study and Parameter Analysis)
章节结构.核心任务: 验证网络核心模块（如双分支结构、自适应模块）的有效性，并探讨和展示关键超参数的最优选择。

章节结构.子节名称: 真实场景评估与局限性讨论 (Real-world Evaluation and Limitation Discussion)
章节结构.核心任务: 展示模型在真实世界非合成图像上的泛化能力，并客观分析方法的弱点、失败案例或适用边界。

Experiments.内容逻辑: 遵循“从宏观到微观、从标准到极端、从外部对比到内部验证”的递进逻辑。首先通过标准数据集上的SOTA对比确立方法的总体优越性（宏观/外部）；接着通过控制变量法深入探究模型在特定挑战（如不同透明度/反射率）下的鲁棒性边界（微观/极端）；随后通过消融实验剖析内部组件的贡献，证明网络设计的合理性（内部验证）；最后回归真实场景并客观探讨局限性，形成完整的科学论证闭环。各子节间的过渡多采用递进连词（如'In addition to...'）或转折/对比句式（如'Although... they still...'）来自然衔接不同维度的实验。
论证手法: 定量与定性结合：使用表格（如MSE, Bad Pix指标）提供严谨的数据支撑，同时辅以可视化图表（如深度图、误差图、局部放大图）进行直观的视觉论证。
控制变量法：在鲁棒性分析中，固定场景其他元素，单一梯度调节特定变量（如透明度从90%降至20%，反射率递减），精准论证模型对单一因素的敏感度。
对比与反衬论证：将本文方法与多种基线方法在相同条件下对比，通过暴露基线方法在特定场景（如非朗伯体、小尺度纹理）下的伪影或几何结构丢失，反衬本文方法的优势。
消融论证：通过移除或替换核心模块（如two-branch structure），对比性能衰减情况，反向证明该模块的不可或缺性。
辩证与客观评价：在展示优势（'The strength of...'）的同时，主动暴露方法的局限性（'The weakness of...'，如前景透明度过低时背景重建困难），增强学术严谨性和可信度。

关键句式模板.实验设置与总体评估: We evaluate our approach through experiments conducted on [Datasets] for comprehensive comparison. / In this section, we report the outcome of our findings concerning...
关键句式模板.与SOTA方法的对比实验: The experimental results demonstrate that our method surpasses state-of-the-art approaches... / As illustrated in Fig. X, the proposed [Method] obtains accurate results under [Condition].
关键句式模板.敏感性与鲁棒性分析: In addition to the comparison on public datasets, in order to explore the sensitivity of the algorithms to [Factor], we conduct a control variable experiment on... / We still use the idea of controlling variables to gradually reduce/increase...
关键句式模板.消融实验与参数分析: We conduct a set of ablation experiments to demonstrate the importance of the [Module/Design]. / Since there are adjustable parameters in [Method], we list the most important optimal parameter selections.
关键句式模板.真实场景评估与局限性讨论: The strength of our method is that it is not significantly affected by [Factor]... / The weakness of our method is that it is challenging to [Task] when [Condition].

章节结构.子节名称: 研究总结与核心贡献
章节结构.核心任务: 回顾研究背景与目的，高度概括本文提出的核心方法、模型、数据集或理论框架，呼应引言部分。

章节结构.子节名称: 具体发现与技术优势
章节结构.核心任务: 详述理论推导发现、关键技术改进点、模型架构创新及其带来的性能提升，突出相较于现有方法的优越性。

章节结构.子节名称: 实验验证与应用价值
章节结构.核心任务: 总结实验结果，强调方法在公开数据集和真实世界数据上的有效性、鲁棒性，以及在实际应用场景中的潜在价值。

章节结构.子节名称: 局限性与未来工作
章节结构.核心任务: 客观指出当前研究在数据、场景或理论上的局限性，并基于这些局限或新发现提出具体的未来研究方向和改进计划。

Conclusion.内容逻辑: 整体呈现“总-分-总”与“过去-现在-未来”相结合的沙漏型逻辑。首先从宏观层面概括全文核心工作（总/过去），接着深入微观的技术细节、理论发现和实验结果以巩固核心贡献（分/现在），最后跳出当前研究边界，指出局限并展望未来研究方向（总/未来）。各子节之间通过语义递进和逻辑连接词（如However, Finally, Meanwhile, To address）进行平滑过渡，从“提出问题/方法”自然流转至“证明方法有效”，再延伸至“未来如何改进”。
论证手法: 归纳总结法：对全文的方法论、实验设置和最终结论进行高度凝练和概括，不引入新信息。
对比论证法：通过与现有基线方法或传统机制进行对比，突出本文方法的独特性和优越性（如“while this is not possible using [5, 6, 7]”、“abandons conventional... instead proposes”）。
事实与数据支撑：引用实验验证的客观事实、数据集规模或具体指标来证明方法的有效性（如“validate our algorithm on most material types”、“26-category thermal adaptation behaviors”）。
因果论证法：基于研究中发现的客观规律或存在的局限性，推导出未来工作的必要性和具体方向（如“since we showed that there is actually inherent ambiguity... future work includes”）。

研究总结与核心贡献: In this paper, we propose a novel [method/framework] for [task/problem] from [data source/context]. (原文示例: In this paper, we propose a novel BRDF-invariant shape and reflectance estimation method for glossy surfaces from light-field cameras.)

To address the core requirements for [problem/domain], this work constructs [dataset], proposes and validates [model] – a framework for [specific task]. (原文示例: To address the core requirements for intelligent detection of human thermal comfort..., this work constructs TABD, proposes and validates TLA-DETR...)
具体发现与技术优势: By utilizing [theory/technique], we show that [theoretical finding/limitation of existing methods]. (原文示例: By utilizing the differential motion theory, we show that direct shape recovery is not possible for general BRDFs.)

According to the characteristics of [data type], multiple key optimization components are incorporated into [module] to effectively enhance [capability]. (原文示例: According to the characteristics of infrared data, multiple key optimization components are incorporated into the encoder/decoder to effectively enhance the model’s spatio-temporal feature extraction...)

To address the uncertainty arising from [issue], [model] abandons conventional [mechanism], instead proposes and adopts a unique [new mechanism] to enhance [performance/credibility]. (原文示例: To address the uncertainty arising from multi-view conflicts, TLA-DETR abandons conventional linear fusion decision mechanisms, instead proposes and adopts a unique multi-view fusion decision mechanism...)
实验验证与应用价值: Experiments validate our algorithm on [dataset types], as well as real-world data taken with [equipment]. (原文示例: Experiments validate our algorithm on most material types in the MERL dataset, as well as real-world data taken with the Lytro Illum.)

A series of experiments conducted on [dataset] validate the superiority of [model] across multiple dimensions. (原文示例: A series of experiments conducted on TABD validate the superiority of TLA-DETR across multiple dimensions.)

Correlation analysis with [ground truth/survey] further corroborates the feasibility of employing [proxy variable] to infer [target metric]. (原文示例: Correlation analysis with survey results further corroborates the feasibility of employing thermal adaptation behaviors as proxy variables to infer subjective thermal perception.)
局限性与未来工作: Finally, since we showed that there is actually inherent [limitation/ambiguity] in [context], future work includes deriving/exploring [future direction]. (原文示例: Finally, since we showed that there is actually inherent ambiguity in light-fields for unknown shape..., future work includes deriving its ambiguity-space...)

Future implementation should focus on integrating [component A] with [component B], where [expected outcome/mechanism]. (原文示例: Future implementation should focus on integrating this behavioral feedback loop with standard PMV calculations, where recognized behaviors dynamically adjust model parameters...)

The current validation is based on [limitation in data/scope]. We will consider [improvement factors] to ensure robust performance across [broader scenarios]. (原文示例: The current validation is based on a limited participant group and a single building context. We will consider individual differences and environmental diversity to ensure robust performance...)

数据集.规模: 包含100种真实测量的BRDF材质样本
数据集.特点: 常用于合成具有复杂非朗伯体（如光泽、金属、塑料）反射特性的光场图像，以评估形状和反射率恢复算法。
数据集.来源论文: SVBRDF-Invariant Shape and Reflectance Estimation from Light-Field Cameras

数据集.规模: 包含大量高分辨率图像及精确的视差/深度Ground Truth
数据集.特点: 专注于透明和镜面（ToM）表面的高难度场景，提供真实标注，用于评估模型对违反朗伯体假设表面的深度估计能力。
数据集.来源论文: Learning Depth Estimation for Transparent and Mirror Surfaces

数据集.规模: 作者自建的包含双层属性（反射层和背景层）的合成光场数据集
数据集.特点: 专门用于解决真实反射场景中难以获取Ground Truth视差图的挑战，支持双层深度的独立评估。
数据集.来源论文: Decoupling and Aggregating: Dual-layer Light Field Depth Estimation with Reflective and Transparent Surfaces

数据集.规模: 包含多个合成场景（如Boxes, Cotton等）和真实世界场景（如Dino, Sideboard等）
数据集.特点: 光场深度估计领域的标准基准，合成场景提供精确GT和EPI序列，真实场景用于验证泛化能力。
数据集.来源论文: Exploiting Sequence Analysis for Accurate Light-Field Depth Estimation

baseline方法.核心思路: 基于传统光场几何和光度一致性假设的经典深度估计方法，依赖朗伯体假设进行多视角匹配。
baseline方法.性能: 在朗伯体区域表现良好，但在非朗伯体/高反光区域误差显著增大，容易产生可见伪影。

baseline方法.核心思路: 基于Vision Transformer架构的单目深度估计模型，通过全局注意力机制捕获上下文信息。
baseline方法.性能: 原始权重在透明/镜面表面深度预测存在严重偏差，通过无监督伪标签微调后显著改善。

baseline方法.核心思路: 基于迭代优化和自适应相关层的立体匹配网络，用于双目/立体深度估计。
baseline方法.性能: 原始模型在ToM区域因反射/折射失效，微调后能大幅提升在复杂表面的鲁棒性和准确度。

baseline方法.名称: 传统EPI特征方法 (Structural Tensor / Parallelogram)
baseline方法.核心思路: 利用EPI中直线斜率与深度成反比的几何原理，通过结构张量、平行四边形算子等手工特征计算视差。
baseline方法.性能: 计算效率高，但对噪声、遮挡和非朗伯体反射导致的EPI线条断裂或交叉（双层场景）非常敏感，精度受限。

评估指标.定义: 预测深度图与真实深度图之间像素级误差的平方均值（或均方根），用于衡量整体深度估计的绝对误差。
评估指标.典型值范围: MSE通常在 0.0001 到 0.05 之间（归一化视差），RMSE在 0.01 到 0.2 之间。

评估指标.定义: 预测视差与真实视差的绝对误差超过特定阈值（如0.07或0.03像素）的像素占总像素的百分比，是光场深度估计最常用的精度指标。
评估指标.典型值范围: 优秀方法的 BadPix0.07 通常低于 5%（合成数据集）或 10%（真实数据集）。

评估指标.定义: 预测深度与真实深度之间绝对误差的平均值，对异常值不如MSE敏感，直观反映平均偏差。
评估指标.典型值范围: 通常在 0.01 到 0.1 像素（视差空间）或具体的深度单位范围内。

figure_style.布局方式: 主体采用自左向右与自上而下结合的级联流式布局；针对双分支/多阶段任务（如成本体解耦、序列分析+优化）采用编码器-解码器对称式或前后端串联架构；监督信号与损失计算模块通常置于网络末端或各解码分支下方，形成清晰的计算拓扑。

模块样式.基础计算块: 圆角矩形，用于卷积、注意力、RNN/LSTM等神经网络层
模块样式.张量/特征数据: 平行四边形或扁平长方体，直观表示空间分辨率与通道数的降采样/上采样变化
模块样式.数学/传统算法模块: 直角矩形或六边形，用于CRF能量优化、数值积分、多项式先验拟合等非可微模块
模块样式.监督与损失节点: 虚线边框矩形，尺寸略小，独立于主干数据流之外便于视觉分离
模块样式.输入/输出接口: 梯形或带切角的矩形，明确标注原始模态（如Light Field/EPI Patch）与最终输出（如Disparity Map/Normal）

连接样式.前向数据流: 黑色实线单向箭头，线宽1.5pt，清晰指示张量传递与特征融合方向
连接样式.跳跃/残差连接: 灰色虚线或点划线，跨越多个层级直接连接编码器下采样路径与解码器上采样路径
连接样式.多阶段监督/损失反馈: 彩色虚线箭头（通常为橙/红色），从中间特征层或分支末端引出，指向对应的伪标签或损失计算单元
连接样式.迭代/循环控制流: 带弧度的弯曲箭头，标注'Iterate'或'Self-guided Mask'，表示模块内部的自引导更新或交替优化过程

配色方案: #3B82F6 (主干特征提取/编码模块主色调)
#10B981 (双分支解耦/聚合路径区分色)
#1F2937 (核心箭头、边框与正文描边色)

标注规范.模块内标注: 采用无衬线字体（如Arial/Helvetica），字号9-10pt；核心操作名居中对齐，关键维度信息（如[H,W,C]或[Batch,Feat,H,W]）以浅灰小字置于模块右下角
标注规范.连接与参数标注: 在箭头中段或转折处直接标注变换类型（如'Conv3×3,stride=2','Concat','UpSample'），保持连线整洁不遮挡模块
标注规范.损失函数位置: 集中标注于架构图底部或右侧独立区块，以标准LaTeX公式呈现（如L_total=L_depth+λL_reflectance），并通过同色系虚线与对应特征层对齐连接
标注规范.层级与图例说明: 复杂架构需在图底提供紧凑图例（Legend），统一解释颜色语义、缩写定义、迭代次数提示及网络超参数（如Channel数、Kernel大小）

7 Conclusions and Future Work
In this paper, we propose a novel BRDF-invariant shape
andreflectanceestimationmethodforglossysurfacesfrom
light-fieldcameras. Byutilizingthedifferentialmotionthe-
ory, we show that direct shape recovery is not possible for
generalBRDFs. However,fora1-lobeBRDFthatdepends
only on half-angle, we derive an SVBRDF-invariant equa-
tionrelatingdepthandnormals.Usingalocallypolynomial
prioronthesurface,shapecanbeestimatedusingthisequa-
tion. Reflectance is then also recovered using our frame-
work. Experiments validate our algorithm on most mate-
rial types in the MERL dataset, as well as real-world data
taken with the Lytro Illum. Spatially-varying BRDFs can
also be handled by our method, while this is not possible
using [5, 6, 7]. Finally, since we showed that there is ac-
tuallyinherentambiguityinlight-fieldsforunknownshape
andgeneralmulti-lobereflectance,futureworkincludesde-
riving its ambiguity-space, i.e., what is the precise set of
shapesandreflectancesthatgeneratesthesamelight-field.

Learning Depth Estimation for Transparent and Mirror Surfaces
AlexCostanzino∗ PierluigiZamaRamirez∗ MatteoPoggi∗
FabioTosi StefanoMattoccia LuigiDiStefano
CVLAB,DepartmentofComputerScienceandEngineering(DISI)
UniversityofBologna,Italy
{alex.costanzino, pierluigi.zama, m.poggi, fabio.tosi5}@unibo.it
https://cvlab-unibo.github.io/Depth4ToM/
Abstract RGB Base Ours
Inferring the depth of transparent or mirror (ToM) sur-
faces represents a hard challenge for either sensors, algo-
rithms,ordeepnetworks. Weproposeasimplepipelinefor
learning to estimate depth properly for such surfaces with
neuralnetworks,withoutrequiringanyground-truthanno-
tation. We unveil how to obtain reliable pseudo labels by
in-paintingToMobjectsinimagesandprocessingthemwith
a monocular depth estimation model. These labels can be
usedtofine-tuneexistingmonocularorstereonetworks,to
letthemlearnhowtodealwithToMsurfaces.Experimental
results on the Booster dataset show the dramatic improve-
mentsenabledbyourremarkablysimpleproposal.
1.Introduction
Inourdailylives,weofteninteractwithseveralobjects
of various appearances. Among them are those made of
transparentormirrorsurfaces(ToM),rangingfromtheglass
windows of buildings to the reflective surfaces of cars and
appliances. These might represent a hard challenge for an
autonomousagentleveragingcomputervisiontooperatein
unknownenvironments.Specifically,amongthemanytasks
involved in Spatial AI, accurately estimating depth infor-
mationonthesesurfacesremainsachallengingproblemfor
both computer vision algorithms and deep networks [64],
yet necessary for proper interaction with the environment
in robotic, autonomous navigation, picking, and other ap-
plicationfields. ThisdifficultyarisesbecauseToMsurfaces
introducemisleadingvisualinformationaboutscenegeom-
etry,whichmakesdepthestimationchallengingnotonlyfor
computer vision systems but even for humans – e.g., we
might not distinguish the presence of a glass door in front
of us due to its transparency. On the one hand, the defini-
tion of depth itself might appearambiguous in such cases:

Exploiting Sequence Analysis for Accurate
Light-Field Depth Estimation

ThisworkwassupportedinpartbytheNationalNaturalScienceFoundationofChinaunderGrant62076122,inpartbytheNatural
ScienceFoundationoftheNanjingInstituteofTechnologyunderGrantZKJ201906,andinpartbytheOpenFoundationfromtheJiangsu
ProvinceEngineeringResearchCenterofIntelliSenseTechnologyandSystemunderGrantITS202101andGrantITS202201.
ABSTRACT Depthestimationforlightfield(LF)imagesisthecornerstoneofmanyapplicationsoflight
fieldcameras,suchas3Dreconstruction,defectsinspection,facelivenessdetection,andsoforth.Inrecent
years, convolutional neural network (CNN) has dominated the primary workhorse for depth estimation.
However, the interpretability of the network and the accuracy of the depth estimation results still need to
beimproved.Thispaperusestheconditionalrandomfield(CRF)theorytoexplainandmodeltheLFdepth
estimation.Further,fromtheperspectiveofsequenceanalysis,weextractthesequencefeaturesofepipolar
planeimage(EPI)patcheswithrecurrentneuralnetwork(RNN)andserveastheunarytermoftheenergy
functionintheCRF.Then,aunifiedneuralnetwork(calledasLFRNN)isdesignedtosolvetheCRFand
get the disparity map. Our LFRNN builds upon two-stage architecture, involving a local depth estimation
andadepthrefinement.Inthefirstpart,wedesignanRNNtoanalyzethevectorsequencesinEPIpatches
and obtain local disparity values. There are two thinking behind the design of this part. The first is the
general principle that the slope of the straight line in the EPI is inversely proportional to the depth; the
secondisouruniqueobservationthatthosestraightlinesaredistributedinvectorsequences.Inthesecond
part, continuous CRF is used to optimize the output of the first part. We train LFRNN on a synthetic LF
datasetandtestitonbothsyntheticandreal-worldLFdatasets.Quantitativeandqualitativeresultsvalidate
thesuperiorperformanceofourLFRNNoverthestate-of-the-artmethods.

We evaluate our approach through experiments conducted
on HCInew, Inria, Stanford, and UrbanLF datasets for
comprehensive comparison. Based on the metrics defined in
[7], the experimental results demonstrate that our method
surpasses state-of-the-art approaches in both synthetic and
real-world scenes. Notably, PlaneNet achieves the top ranking
ontheHCI4DLightFieldbenchmark,outperformingothersin
terms of mean square error (MSE). Additionally, we further
explore the utilization of our representation in multiple LF
depthestimationnetworks,andexperimentsconfirmimproved
performance when continuous surface is applied.
The contributions of this paper are as follows:
• Surface-continuous Scene Representation. We propose
a novel surface-continuous scene representation that
incorporates planarity priors, enabling an approximate
description of real-world surfaces and overcoming the
limitations of discrete image representations.
• Plane Regular Sampling Operator (PRSO). We intro-
duce PRSO for plane digitization, which allows the
network to learn continuous signals effectively without
explicit plane parameterization. Additionally, we extend
PRSO to various LF depth estimation networks, demon-
strating enhanced performance when our representation
is applied.

TODE-Trans: Transparent Object Depth Estimation with Transformer
Kang Chen*1, Shaochen Wang*1, Beihao Xia2, Dongxu Li1, Zhen Kan1, and Bin Li1

automation and daily life. However, robust visual recognition Depth camera

Transfer learning-aided DETR framework for multi-view indoor infrared human
thermal adaptation behavior recognition and location
WenruiZhu a,JunqiYu b,c,∗,DonghuiShi d,FangnanDonge,RuifengHuangf,JunmengSongg
aSchoolofCivilEngineering,Xi’anUniversityofArchitectureandTechnology,No.13YantaRoad,Xi’an,710055,Shaanxi,China
bSchoolofBuildingServicesScienceandEngineering,Xi’anUniversityofArchitectureandTechnology,No.13Yanta
Road,Xi’an,710055,Shaanxi,China
cXAUATEngineeringCo.,ltd.,No.13YantaRoad,Xi’an,710055,Shaanxi,China
dSchoolofElectronicsandInformationEngineering,AnhuiJianzhuUniversity,No.292ZiyunRoad,Hefei,230601,Anhui,China
eSchoolofManagement,Xi’anUniversityofArchitectureandTechnology,No.13YantaRoad,Xi’an,710055,Shaanxi,China
fHangzhouInternationalInnovationInstitute,BeihangUniversity,No.166ShuanghongqiaoRoad,Hangzhou,311115,Zhejiang,China
gHefeiGocomInformationTechnologyCo.,ltd.,Hefei,230061,Anhui,China
Abstract
Applying computer vision technology for human thermal adaptation behavior and location in the building indoor
environmentenablesreal-timemonitoringandfeedbackofthermalcomfortlevels. Toovercomevariouschallenges
associated with RGB-based methods, a transfer learning-aided detection Transformer framework (TLA-DETR) is
proposedformulti-viewinfraredindoorhumanthermaladaptationbehaviorrecognitionandlocation.Thisframework
effectivelybridgesthesubstantialmodalitygapbetweenvisible-lightandinfrareddomainsthroughanoveltwo-stage
transfertrainingparadigm. IntheTransformerencoder/decoder,relativepositionalencodingisintroducedtoenhance
spatial awareness and the Fourier-modulated attention module is embedded to suppress noise. To make reliable
decisionsfrommulti-viewinputs,afusiondecisionmechanismbasedonoptimizedYagerruleisdesignedtomodel
conflicting evidence. This work conducts multiple experiments and surveys within a commercial office building in
Xi’an,China. ResultsdemonstratethattheTLA-DETRframeworkachievesstate-of-the-artperformanceinthermal
adaptationbehaviorrecognitionandlocation.
Keywords: Humanthermaladaptationbehavior,Transferlearning,DETR,Infraredvideounderstanding,Indoor
thermalcomfort,Behaviorrecognitionandlocation
1. Introduction
Inmodernsociety,globalclimatechangeandenergyscarcityareincreasinglyacutechallenges. Buildingenergy
consumptionconstitutesoneoftheprimarysourcesofglobalenergyexpenditure,accountingforapproximatelyone-
thirdoftotalworldwideenergyusage[1]. Some40%oftotalbuildingenergyconsumptionisattributabletoheating,
ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems [2]. Despite this substantial energy expenditure, many occupants
remaindissatisfiedwiththebuilding’sindoorthermalenvironment.
Withtheintensificationofurbanization,modernofficeworkersengagedincommercialactivitiesnowspendover
90%oftheirdailytimeindoors. Thismakestheregulationofindoorthermalconditionsacriticalfactorinfluencing
health and well-being [3]. Excessively high indoor temperatures or inadequate climate control can trigger various
health issues, such as cardiovascular and respiratory diseases [4, 5], all of which are closely associated with indoor
thermalcomfort[6].
Toensureindividualthermalcomfortwhilesimultaneouslyachievingenergyconservationandemissionsreduc-
tion, traditional constant-temperature setting strategies are gradually being replaced by occupant-centric adaptive
∗Correspondingauthor
Emailaddresses:wenruizhu_96@xauat.edu.cn(WenruiZhu ),junqi@xauat.edu.cn(JunqiYu )
controlapproaches[7]. Recentstudieshaveemphasizedthecriticalroleofoccupantbehaviormodelinginbuilding
performancesimulationandthermalcomfortassessment[8,9]. Forinstance,adaptivemodelsbasedonfieldsurveys
havesuccessfullylinkedspecificbehaviors(e.g.,windowopening)tothermalsensationandenergyuse[10].Thecore
principle of such methods is dynamically adjusting indoor environmental parameters based on real-time perception
ofoccupants’actualthermalsensations. However,conventionalintrusiveorsemi-intrusiveapproachesforacquiring
objective and subjective thermal sensation data exhibit inherent limitations in thermal comfort assessment [11]. In-
trusiveapproachesincludequestionnairesurveys,physiologicalsignalmeasurements,andenvironmentalmonitoring.
Questionnairesurveysarehighlyintrusive,andthepersistent,frequentrequestsforuserfeedbackcandisruptpartic-
ipants’ normal daily lives and work [12]. Physiological signal measurement requires sensors to be placed in direct
contactwithhumanskin,withthesensationofaforeignobjectandrestrictedmobilitybeingtheprimaryobstaclesto
suchmeasurements[13].Themainchallengesforenvironmentalparametersensorsincludespatialpositionaldiscrep-
anciesbetweenthesensorsandoccupants,aswellastheunevendistributionofindoortemperature,windspeed,and
illuminancelevels[14,15]. Semi-intrusiveapproachesprimarilyrelyonwearabledevicessuchassmartwristbands
[16,17],glasses[18],orwatches[19]. Comparedtoinvasivemeasurements,semi-invasivemeasurementscauseless
disruptiontoanindividual’sbehavior,thoughtheystillrequirethewearingofequipment.
Non-invasiveapproachesoffergreatercomfortandimperceptibilitytooccupants. Currentnon-invasivemeasure-
ments primarily utilize infrared (IR) [20–22] or visible-light (RGB) [23–25] cameras to capture temperature, heat
flux,orotherphysiologicalsignalsfromtheskinsurfaceofexposedbodyregions,therebyassessingindividualther-
malcomfort.However,theseapproachesoftenoverlooktheinfluenceofphysicalactivityonphysiologicalparameters
duringnon-staticstates,andindividualsubjectivethermalperceptionsmaynotalwaysbereflectedinmeasurableob-
jectiveindicators.
Compared to temperature, heat flux, or other physiological signals, the instinctive thermal adaptation behaviors
exhibitedbythehumanbodyreflectitsadjustmenttovaryinglevelsofthermalcomfortfrombothobjectiveandsub-
jective perspectives, which are more readily perceived and captured. Prior to visual approaches, extensive research
hasexploredsensor-basedoccupancybehavioralpatternrecognitionusingambientsensors. Forinstance,Dongand
Andrews[26]developedambientsensor-basedmodellingusingacoustics, CO2, andmotiondatatopredictuserbe-
haviorforenergyandcomfortmanagement,demonstratingpotentialenergysavingsof30%. Multi-sensordatafusion
approacheshavefurtherbeenappliedtodecodeoccupantbehaviorinsharedspacesofopen-planofficesbyintegrating
Wi-FiandBluetoothpositioningwithenvironmentaldata[27]. Additionally,sensor-fusion-basedclusteringanalysis
hasbeenusedtorevealoccupantbehaviorpatternsinuniversitybuildingoffices[28].However,thesenon-visualmeth-
odsoftenstruggletodistinguishspecificthermaladaptationbehaviorcategories. Advancesincomputervisionhave
enabledautomatedrecognitionofindoorhumanthermaladaptationbehaviors,offeringnewpossibilitiesforoccupant-
centric building control. These studies primarily relied on RGB surveillance videos, employing skeleton extraction
or spatiotemporal feature models to classify behaviors [29–31]. Although thermal adaptation behavior recognition
methodsbasedonindoorRGBsurveillancevideoshavedevelopedrapidly,twocriticalchallengesremainunresolved.
First,theanalysisofindoorRGBsurveillancevideosraisessignificantconcernsregardingpersonalprivacy[30]. To
address this, recent research has shifted toward non-intrusive thermal adaptation behavior recognition using Wi-Fi
channelstateinformation(CSI)[32,33]. Whiletheseapproachescircumventprivacy-relatedcontroversies,theirper-
formanceishighlysusceptibletodegradationwhenWi-Fisignalsaresubjecttointerference. Second,thedataquality
of RGB videos is affected by ambient lighting conditions and illuminance levels within indoor environments [34].
Reflectionsofindoorlightduringdailyhoursandpoorvisibilitycausedbyinadequateindoorlightduringnighthours
canbothdegradethedataqualityofRGBvideos,therebyadverselyaffectingtheperformanceofbehaviorrecognition.
Theadoptionofindoorinfraredsurveillancevideoscaneffectivelydealwiththetwocriticalchallengesmentioned
above. Infrareddataprimarilycapturesthethermalsilhouetteofthehumanbody,renderingitvirtuallyincapableof
discerningsubtlefacialdifferences. ComparedtoRGBdata,infrareddataismoreprivacy-friendly[14]. Ontheother
hand,theprincipleandmechanismofinfraredthermalimaginginherentlydetermineitsinsensitivitytoindoorlight-
ing and illuminance levels [35]. Nevertheless, existing research on thermal adaptation behavior recognition rarely
employsinfrareddata, owingtotwointrinsiclimitationsassociatedwithIRmodalities. First, constrainedbyacqui-
sition costs, high-quality and well-annotated infrared video datasets are exceedingly scarce [36]. This necessitates
infraredhumanbehaviorrecognitionmodelstopossessoutstandingfew-shotlearningcapabilities. Althoughexten-
siveindoorRGBsurveillancevideodatasetsarepubliclyavailable,thesignificantmodalitygapbetweenvisible-light
and infrared images renders direct transfer learning ineffective [37]. Second, infrared thermal imaging is prone to
2
noiseinterferencecausedbyenvironmentalfactorsandtheimagingequipmentitself[18]. Whilemaintainingastable
thermalenvironmentisachievablethroughcontrolledHVACsystemstokeepindoortemperaturesconstant,noiseand
pixel-leveldistortionsininfrareddatageneratedbyimagingequipmentremainunavoidable. Mitigatingtheimpactof
datanoisepresentsatechnicalchallengefortherobustnessofthermaladaptationbehaviorrecognitionmodels.
Beyondthelimitationsinherenttotheinfrareddataitself,relatedworksonthermaladaptationbehaviorrecognition
exhibitcertaincommonlimitations. Forinstance, scarcelyanyresearchhasexploredthespatiallocationofthermal
adaptationbehaviors.Inpatternrecognitiontasks,accuratelocationisascriticalasclassification.Forthermalcomfort
regulationinlargeindoorspaces,locationinformationenablesHVACsystemstodeterminetheexactpositionwhere
activities occur, thereby providing the basis for accurate temperature and humidity control in localized zones. On
theotherhand,acriticaltechnicalgapremainsregardingmulti-viewvisualinformationfusioninbuildingoperation
contexts. Whilenumerousrelatedworkshavereportedcollectingmulti-viewthermaladaptationbehaviordatafrom
indoorsurveillancevideos,noneofthemconsiderintegratingmulti-viewinformationtoresolveconflictsindetection
resultsbetweendifferentviews. Inreal-worldindoorsurveillancescenarios,infraredvideodatafrommultipleangles
oftenexhibitsignificantvariationsinthermalsilhouettesduetoviewdiscrepancies. Thisleadstoconflictingbehavior
classificationopinionsandlocationboundingboxpredictionsacrossviews. Existingmulti-sourceinformationfusion
decision-making strategies predominantly rely on traditional linear fusion decisions (majority voting or weighted
average sorting), which are based upon the assumption that evidence from each information source is independent
and equally reliable. However, in practical scenarios, significant discrepancies exist in the confidence levels and
spatialconsistencyacrossdifferentviews. Particularlywhenexcessiveconflictingpredictionsarisefrommulti-view
infrareddata,simplelinearfusiondecisionsyieldahighprobabilityofmisclassification. Therefore,auniquefusion
decisionmechanismformulti-viewindoorinfraredbehaviorrecognitionandlocationisrequiredtoexplicitlymodel
uncertaintyandappropriatelyhandlemulti-conflictevidence.
To address the aforementioned challenges and fill certain research gaps, this paper constructs a large-scale in-
frareddatasetencompassing26categoriesofthermaladaptationbehaviorsinSection2(Data),andproposesatransfer
learning-aided DETR framework (TLA-DETR) in Section 3 (Methodology). TLA-DETR adopts a novel two-stage
transfertrainingparadigm,incorporatesrelativepositionalencodingandFouriermodulationattention(FMA)module
[38]withintheTransformerencoder/decoder. Ourlatestwork[39]exploredpreliminaryeffortstoapplymulti-source
uncertaintyfusiondecisiontheorytoskeleton-basedactionrecognitiontasks. Inspiredbythiswork,thispaperherein
designs a multi-view fusion decision mechanism based on Yager rules [40] specifically tailored for infrared human
thermaladaptationbehaviorrecognition. InSection4(Experiments)andAppendixA/B,thebehaviorrecognitionand
location performance of TLA-DETR are objectively evaluated by multiple dimensions. These experimental dimen-
sionsincludesensitivityanalysisofkeyhyperparameters, visualizationresultanalysis, ablationstudy, decisioncase
analysis, robustnesstest, andcomparativeexperiment. InSection5(InvestigationandDiscussion), furtherresearch
is conducted into the correlation between individuals’ subjective thermal sensations and their indoor thermal adap-
tationbehaviorpatterns. Itisimportanttoclarifythattheproposedbehaviorrecognitionframeworkisnotintended
to replace established physical models like PMV, but rather to complement them by providing real-time behavioral
feedbackthatrefinesmodelinputs.
Thespecificcontributionsofthispapercanbedetailedasfollows.
• constructs an indoor infrared thermal adaptation behavior dataset with 26 categories of thermal adaptation
behaviors(12categoriesofheat-sensation-relatedbehaviorsand14categoriesofcold-sensation-relatedbehav-
iors). Tothebestofourknowledge,thisdatasetrepresentsthemostcomprehensiveinfrareddatasetuntilnow
forthistask.
• proposestheTLA-DETRframeworkwithtwo-stagetransfertrainingparadigmtobridgethedatamodalitygap,
achievingrobusttrainingperformancewithminimalinfrareddatasamples.
• introduces relative positional encoding within the Transformer encoder/decoder to enhance the spatial aware-
ness of multi-head self-attention mechanisms and FMA modules within the TLA-DETR encoder/decoder to
suppressdatanoise.
• designsamulti-viewfusiondecisionmechanismbasedontheoptimizedYagerrule. Itperformstwiceiterative
fusionbetweenthreeviews’classificationopinionsfromthesameinfrareddatasample,therebyenhancingthe
accuracyandreliabilityofthefinaldecision.
3
• TLA-DETRachievesstate-of-the-artbehaviorrecognitionaccuracy(94.2%)onthetestset.Itsbehaviorlocation
accuracy(88.1%)alsosurpassesmostmainstreammethodsonthetestset. Consideringexperimentalresultsof
otherdimensions,theimprovementsofTLA-DETRarebotheffectiveandreasonable.
• throughaquestionnairesurveyconductedwithinacommercialofficebuildinginXi’an,China,weinvestigate
thecorrelationbetweenindividuals’subjectivethermalsensationsandtheirindoorthermaladaptationbehavior
patterns. Randomsamplesofthetop-1typicalthermaladaptationbehaviorassociatedwith6subjectivethermal
sensationcategoriesarechosenandtestedbyTLA-DETR.Theresultsfurthervalidatedthemodel’sreliability.
2. Data
2.1. RelatedDatasets
Basedonthermaladaptationbehaviordatasetsfrommultiplerelatedworks[29,30,41–43],asummaryofvarious
humanbehaviorsassociatedwithheatsensationsandcoldsensationsispresentedinTable1.Here,B001–B012denote
numbersofheat-sensation-relatedbehaviors,whileB013–B026denotenumbersofcold-sensation-relatedbehaviors.
Thesymbol"✓"indicatesthatthedatasetincludesthecorrespondingbehaviorcategory. Amongallrelevantstudies,
the dataset constructed by Duan et al. [29] contains the most thermal adaptation behavior categories, totalling 16.
In this paper, we propose a new Thermal Adaptation Behavior Dataset (TABD), which extends these 16 behaviors
by adding 4 additional heat-sensation-related behaviors (Swing hair, Stretch limbs, Take off gloves, and Pull away
the collar) and 6 cold-sensation-related behaviors (Pull the collar tight, Put on gloves, Put hands in pockets, Cross
one’slegs,Ringhandsaroundneck,andShivering). TABDencompassesatotalof26behaviorcategories,covering
all behaviors listed in the aforementioned referenced datasets and significantly increasing the challenge of thermal
adaptationbehaviorrecognitiontasks.
The PMV (Predicted Mean Vote) index is an indicator used to quantify thermal sensation within the thermal
comfortpredictionmodelproposedbyFangeretal. [44]. Basedonthehumanheatbalanceequation,thePMVindex
integrates four physical variables: air temperature, air velocity, mean radiant temperature, and relative humidity. It
categorizesindoorthermalsensationintosevenlevels:cold(-3),cool(-2),slightlycool(-1),neutral(0),slightlywarm
(+1), warm (+2), and hot (+3). The American Society of Heating, Refrigerating, and Air-Conditioning Engineers
(ASHRAE) has adopted the PMV index in Standard 55 [45] to guide the calculation and assessment of thermal
comfort. Concurrently, ISO 7730 [46] recommends maintaining the PMV index within the range of 0 ± 0.5 as the
ideal thermal comfort level. Building upon prior research by Duan et al. [29], we further establish correlations
between26thermaladaptationbehaviorsandthePMVindex,aspresentedinTable1.
2.2. DataCollectionDetails
TheinfrareddatainTABDarecollectedfromthereal-worldindoorenvironmentofacommercialofficebuilding
inXi’an,China. Differentlevelsofthermalenvironments(bothheatandcold)aresimulatedbypreciselycontrolling
thetemperature,airflow,andhumidityoftheHVACsystem,asdetailedinTable2. Theparticipantsinclude4work-
place volunteers in an indoor office environment. To ensure the representativeness of the sample while minimizing
confounding factors from inter-individual differences, we carefully select participants with quantified demographic
and physiological characteristics, as summarized in Table 2a. Specifically, the participant group comprises 2 males
and2females,withagesrangingfrom24to38years(mean: 31.0±6.2years),bodymassindices(BMI)withinthe
normal range of 18.9–24.3 kg/m² (mean: 21.8 ± 2.3 kg/m²), and self-reported metabolic rates consistent with light
officework(1.0–1.2met). Allparticipantsarenon-smokers, havenoknownthermoregulatorydisorders, andreport
similarthermalpreferencepatternsinpreliminaryscreeningquestionnaires. Participantswearstandardizedofficeat-
tire(cottonshirt,trousers/skirt,andlightshoes)withclothinginsulationvaluesconstrainedwithin0.5–0.7clo. This
rangerepresentstypicalsummerofficeclothingandreducesthermalsignaturevariabilityduetogarmentmaterialor
layeringdifferences. Beforeeachdatacollectionsession, participantsareexplicitlyinformedoftherequiredmove-
ments and angles. To fully simulate indoor thermal adaptation behaviors, all behavioral recordings are conducted
andcollectedunderexpertsupervisiontoensureconsistentexecutionintermsofmovementamplitude,duration,and
repetitionfrequency. Eachparticipantcompletessimulationsofall26behaviorcategories, with150shots(450raw
videoclipswith3–8secondsinduration)percategory.
4

3. Methodology
TLA-DETR employs a two-stage cross-domain transfer learning framework, aiming to address the challenge of
efficientdetectionandlocationofindoorthermaladaptationbehaviorsunderinfrareddatascarcity(few-shotlearning
conditions).Itscoreprincipleinvolvespre-trainingarobustfeatureextractionanddetectionarchitectureonlarge-scale
visible-light datasets, then transferring visual information to the infrared domain through a unique transfer learning
trick (freezing the CNN backbone + parameter sharing of the Transformer encoder/decoder + fine-tuning only the
FFNs)toachieveefficientdomainadaptation.
3.1. OverallArchitecture
TheoverallarchitectureoftheproposedTLA-DETRisillustratedinFigure2. ThisapproachemploysDETRas
its baseline framework and comprises three key components: a feature extractor, an object encoder/decoder, and a
predictionhead.
ThefeatureextractorcomprisesaCNNbackbone,a1x1convolutionallayer,andpositionalencoding. First,the
input raw image (3 × H × W) is fed into a CNN backbone (ResNet-50 [48]), which maps it to a high-dimensional
featuremap(2048×H/32×W/32). Thisfeaturemapissubsequentlyreducedindimensionviaa1x1convolutional
7
layer to yield a low-dimensional, high-semantic input representation (256 × H/32 × W/32). To encode spatial con-
text,automaticallygeneratedanddimensionallyalignedlearnablepositionalencodingsareelement-wiseaddedtothe
featuremap,therebyembeddingexplicitpositionalinformationforeachfeaturevector.
Thefeaturemapdimensions(H/32×W/32×256)arereshapedforinputintotheTransformerencoder,yieldingan
outputfeaturemapofidenticalsize(encoderoutputfeature). N objectqueriesareconstructedwith256-dimensional
learnableembeddingvectorsaspriorinformationfortheTransformerdecoder.
Thepredictionheadcomprisesmultipleparallel,non-weight-sharingFeed-ForwardNeuralNetworks(FFNs),each
responsible for mapping the output features of the Transformer decoder to the target classifications (N × classnum)
andpositionregressions(N×4). Theregressionresultsencompassthenormalizedcoordinates, width, andheightof
thepredictedboundingbox.

3.3. Encoder/DecoderDesignofTLA-DETR
Oneofthecorechallengesinindoorthermaladaptationbehaviorrecognitionliesinthefactthatbehaviorpatterns
depend on spatial relationships with long-range temporal sequences (e.g., "pull away the collar—take off clothes").
ComparedtotraditionalCNN-basedmodels, Transformer-basedarchitectures(e.g., DETR[49]anditsvariants)are
better suited for modeling these global, long-distance semantic dependencies. Concurrently, behavior recognition
via infrared imaging also faces challenges like ambiguous target (human) morphology (lack of geometric texture)
and complex backgrounds (interference from thermal sources and noise). For these, we specifically design the en-
coder/decoder architecture of TLA-DETR, as shown in Figure 3 (a). The encoder/decoder innovatively adopts a
multi-headself-attentionmodule(MHA)enhancedwithrelativepositionalencodingtolearngeometricpriors,thereby
overcomingthelimitationsofabsolutepositionalencodingininfraredhumanbehaviorrecognition. Simultaneously,
aFourierModulationAttention(FMA)[38]moduleisintroducedtoactivelysuppressnoisyfrequencycomponents
andenhancethelearningofglobalthermalradiationdistributionpatternsininfraredimages.
TheencoderofTLA-DETRconsistsofN identicalencoderlayersstackedsequentially. Eachlayerreceivesthe
feature map (H × W × C) extracted from the front-end CNN, which is first flattened into a sequence form (HW ×
C) and added with relative positional encoding to preserve two-dimensional spatial information. Subsequently, this
sequenceisfedintotheMHAandFMAmodulestocaptureglobalcontextsatlowcomputationalcost. Finally, the
outputismappedthroughtheFFNmodule(typicallycomposedoftwolinearlayerswithGELU activationfunction)
intoasetofimagefeaturerepresentationsrichinglobalsemanticsandspatialrelationshipsforusebythedecoder.Itis
worthnotingthateachmoduleissequentiallyprocessedthroughresidualconnections(Add)andlayernormalization
(Norm)topreventdegradationindeeplayers.
ThedecoderofTLA-DETRalsoconsistsofM identicaldecoderlayersstackedsequentially. Itsinputcomprises
two components: first, the image feature representations from the encoder; second, learnable object queries, the
number of which equals the predefined maximum number of detectable objects. Each object query represents a
candidaterepresentationofapotentialtarget,withitsinitialvaluebeingarandomlyinitializedvector.First,theobject
queriesstillundergofeatureextractionviatheMHAandFMAmodules,subsequentlyconcatenatingthesewithimage
features output by the encoder to dynamically focus on the regions within the image most relevant to the current
9
query. ThefusedfeaturesaresimilarlyprocessedthroughtheM-layeriterativedecodingprocesstoproduceasetof
refinedobjectqueries. Eachrefinedqueryisfinallyfedintothepredictionhead(comprisingaclassificationheadand
aboundingboxregressionhead)togeneratethefinalbehaviorrecognitionandlocationresults.
3.3.1. MHAModuleEnhancedwithRelativePositionEncoding
In infrared images, the thermal silhouette of the human body is the sole cue for behavior recognition. While
lackingthetextureandcolorpresentinvisiblelight,itsspatialgeometricrelationshipsarehighlystable. Forinstance,
the head, torso, and limbs of a person always exhibit specific relative positional distributions in infrared thermal
imaging,whilebehaviorslikeraisinghandsorstompingfeetaltertherelativedistancesandanglesamongthesebody
parts. The sinusoidal spatial positional encoding employed by standard Transformers is absolute (based on pixel
coordinates within the image), yet in indoor environments, the position of human behaviors within the image may
undergosignificantdisplacement. Themodelrequiresrelativerelationshipsratherthanabsolutecoordinates. Relative
positionalencodingenablesthemodeltolearninvariantgeometricpriorssuchas"theheadisalwaysapproximately20
pixelsabovethetorso"or"thehandsaretypicallylocatedoneithersideofthetorso". Thissignificantlyenhancesthe
model’srobustnesstoposevariationsandpositionaldisplacement.Whenthemodeldetectsone“heatplaque”directly
above another, it can immediately leverage prior knowledge encoded by relative positional encoding to determine
that this configuration is more likely to represent a “person” rather than “other heat sources”. This geometry-based
learnablerelativerelationshipmodellingisthekeytodistinguishingsubtlebehaviorsininfraredimages.
The usage of relative position encoding for attention weight computation constitutes a refined enhancement of
theconventionalMHAmechanism. Thismoduleisdesignedtoexplicitlyintroducerelativespatialpositionalpriors

overcomingthedeficiencyingeometricperceptioncausedbyrelyingsolelyonabsolutepositionencodinginstandard
attention mechanisms. Its embedding process is illustrated in Figure 3 (b). First, the feature sequence is linearly
projected into three tensors: query (Q), key (K), and value (V). The projection matrices are implemented as 1 × 1
convolutions(WQ,WK,WV),maintainingconsistentprojectiondimensions(H×W×C).Relativepositionalencoding
encodestherelativetwo-dimensionaloffsetbetweentwopixels(x, x )viaanembeddingtable,generatingarelative

4. Experiments
4.1. ImplementationDetails
The experiments for TLA-DETR involve a two-stage training process. As shown in Figure 2, the model is first

4.3. Cross-SubjectGeneralizationTest
WhiletheprimaryexperimentsinSection4.2validatethemodel’sperformanceunderastandardclip-leveldata
partition (where all participants are represented in training and test sets), a critical question remains regarding the
model’sabilitytogeneralizetounseenindividuals. Inreal-worldbuildingdeployments, thesystemmustaccurately
recognizebehaviorsfromoccupantswhoarenotpartoftheinitialtrainingdataset. Toaddressthisconcernandevalu-
atetherobustnessofTLA-DETRagainstinter-individualdifferences(e.g.,bodyshape,clothinginsulation,metabolic
rates, andbehavioralhabits), weconductanadditionalcross-subjectgeneralizationtestusingaLeave-One-Subject-
Out(LOSO)protocol.
InthisLOSOsetup,thedatasetispartitionedbasedonparticipantidentityratherthanvideoclips. Specifically,for
eachiteration,datafromthreeparticipantsareusedfortrainingandvalidation,whiledatafromtheremainingheld-out
participant was reserved exclusively for testing. This process is repeated four times, ensuring that each participant
servesasthetestsubjectexactlyonce. Itisimportanttonotethatthegroup-awareclip-levelsplitstrategydescribed
inSection2.3isstillenforcedwithineachfoldtopreventmulti-viewdataleakage.
Theresultsofthecross-subjectgeneralizationtestaresummarizedinTable4. Theaveragerecognitionaccuracy
across the four folds is 89.5%, with a standard deviation of 1.8%. The average location accuracy (Avg IoU) is
87.8%,withastandarddeviationof0.2%. Thisdegradationisexpectedandprimarilyattributabletothedomainshift
caused by individual physiological and behavioral variations that were not present in the training set. For instance,
participants with higher body mass indices may exhibit different thermal radiation patterns, and gender differences
mayleadtodistinctbehavioralpreferencesforthesamethermalsensation.
Despite this decline, the LOSO results demonstrate that TLA-DETR retains a high level of reliability (nearly
90%accuracy)evenwhendeployedonunseenoccupants. Thisconfirmsthatthetwo-stagetransferlearningstrategy
successfully learns generalizable visual features from the source RGB domain that are robust to individual differ-
ences. Forbuildingengineeringapplications,thislevelofgeneralizationissufficientforinitialdeployment,wherethe
modelcanprovideimmediatezone-levelcontrolfeedback. Furthermore,thisresulthighlightsthepotentialvalueof
futureonlinelearningmechanisms,wherethemodelcouldbefine-tunedincrementallyasnewoccupantdatabecomes
availableinthespecificbuildingenvironment.

WhiletheproposedTLA-DETRframeworkdemonstratesstate-of-the-artperformanceontheTABDdataset,sev-
eral limitations regarding generalizability must be acknowledged to contextualize these results for broader building
engineeringapplications. First,thedatasetiscollectedfromonlyfourparticipantswithinasinglecommercialoffice
building undercontrolled HVACconditions. Althoughthis controlledsetup ensuresdata qualityfor algorithmvali-
dation,itmaynotfullycapturethediversityofreal-worldoccupantpopulations. Inter-individualdifferences,suchas

tion efficiency. Similarly, cultural differences in thermal adaptation behaviors (e.g., preference for wearing layers
vs. adjustingthermostats)mayintroducedomainshiftswhendeployingthemodelindifferentgeographicalregions.
Second,environmentaldiversityremainsaconstraint. Thecurrentstudyisconductedinaspecificofficeenvironment
withconsistentbackgroundthermalproperties. Inrealisticdeployments,dynamicbackgroundheatsources(e.g.,di-
rectsunlight,operatingequipment,orvaryingwalltemperatures)couldintroduceadditionalnoisethatdiffersfromthe
controlled training conditions. While the Fourier Modulated Attention (FMA) module enhances robustness against
standardnoise,extremeenvironmentalvariationsmaystillleadtoperformancedegradation. Therefore,theclaimed
state-of-the-art performance should be interpreted within the boundary conditions of this study. The TLA-DETR
framework is validated primarily for office-like environments with similar thermal settings. The detailed analysis

6. Conclusion
Toaddressthecorerequirementsforintelligentdetectionofhumanthermalcomfortinindoorbuildingenviron-
ments, this work constructs TABD, proposes and validates TLA-DETR – a framework for identifying and locating
humanthermaladaptationbehaviorsbasedoninfrareddata. TheestablishmentofTABDfillsthegapinhigh-quality
annotateddataforthefieldofinfraredthermaladaptationbehaviorrecognition. TLA-DETRachieveshigh-accuracy
recognitionandlocationof26-categorythermaladaptationbehaviors(12heatsensation-relatedbehaviorsand14cold
sensation-related behaviors). This model performs efficient and stable training on extremely limited infrared data,
fullydemonstratingthepotentialofcross-domainfeaturetransferinfew-shotlearning. Accordingtothecharacteris-
tics of infrared data, multiple key optimization components are incorporated into the encoder/decoder to effectively
enhancethemodel’sspatio-temporalfeatureextractionandanti-interferencecapabilities. Toaddresstheuncertainty
arisingfrommulti-viewconflicts,TLA-DETRabandonsconventionallinearfusiondecisionmechanisms,insteadpro-
poses and adopts a unique multi-view fusion decision mechanism based on multi-source information fusion theory
toenhancethecredibilityofthedecision-makingprocess. AseriesofexperimentsconductedonTABDvalidatethe
superiorityofTLA-DETRacrossmultipledimensions. Correlationanalysiswithsurveyresultsfurthercorroborates
the feasibility of employing thermal adaptation behaviors as proxy variables to infer subjective thermal perception.
Future implementation should focus on integrating this behavioral feedback loop with standard PMV calculations,
whererecognizedbehaviorsdynamicallyadjustmodelparameterstoachievehigherassessmentaccuracy.
The current validation is based on a limited participant group and a single building context. We will consider
individual differences and environmental diversity to ensure robust performance across varied building scenarios,
andstrivetoexplorebroaderapplicationsofTLA-DETRwithinintelligentbuildingenvironmentalcontrolsystems.
Meanwhile,integratinginfrareddatawithothersensordatatoconstructamorecomprehensivehumanthermalcomfort
assessment framework, aiming to thoroughly quantify subjective human thermal sensation via objective evaluation
results.

To demonstrate the practical implications of the proposed TLA-DETR framework for building operation and
translateTLA-DETR’srecognitionoutputsintomeasurableoperationalbenefitsrelativetoabaselinecontrolstrategy,
thisappendixpresentsanillustrativeengineeringsimulationfocusingonzone-levelHVACcontrolbasedonlocalized
cold-relatedbehaviordetection. Aone-weekfieldpilotstudywasconductedinthesamecommercialofficebuilding
wheretheTABDdatasetwascollected. Thisstudycomparesthetheoreticalprojectionswithactualfieldtestresults
(as shown in Table D.16) to evaluate the practical feasibility and performance gaps of the behavior-driven HVAC
controlstrategy.
Scenario and Requirement: A 1000 m2 indoor office space divided into 10 thermal zones, each monitored by
independent infrared camera nodes (a thermal zone requires three infrared cameras with different views, an edge
computingdevicelikeNVIDIAJetsonAGXforreal-timeinference,andaBACnetorModbusprotocolinterfacefor
communication with existing BMS) running the TLA-DETR model. Each zone accommodates approximately 1-2
occupants during standard operating hours (8:00-18:00). For the field pilot study, two adjacent zones (Zone A and
ZoneB)withsimilarenvelopecharacteristicswereselectedfordetailedcomparison.

It is worth noting that while the field test results validate the positive trend of the theoretical model, certain
deviationsareobserved. Real-worldbenefitsmayvaryduetothefollowingfactors.
• HVAC System Inertia: Thermal systems have delayed response times, which may reduce the effectiveness of
frequent setpoint adjustments. Thermal systems have delayed response times, which may reduce the effec-
tivenessoffrequentsetpointadjustments. Thetheoreticalmodelassumedinstantsetpointadjustments,butthe
actualheatingsystemrequired15-20minutestostabilizeafterasetpointchange. Thisdelayoccasionallyledto
minorovershooting,reducingnetefficiency.
• OccupantAdaptationLatency: Occupantsmaynotimmediatelyperceiveorrespondtoenvironmentalchanges.
Survey feedback indicated occupants did not immediately perceive comfort improvements after the system
adjusted, and some behavioral responses (e.g., putting on clothes) were habitual rather than purely thermal-
driven,leadingtooccasionalfalsetriggers.
• Building Envelope Characteristics: Insulation quality, window-to-wall ratio, and external weather conditions
significantlyinfluenceactualenergyconsumption.
• Model Generalization: Performance may degrade when deployed in buildings with different environmental
conditionsoroccupantdemographics. Therecognitionandlocationaccuracyinthefieldisslightlylowerthan
thecontrolledtestsetduetounseenenvironmentalvariations.
Future work will involve coupling TLA-DETR with Building Energy Modeling (BEM) tools (e.g., EnergyPlus)
forfull-yearsimulationandfielddeploymenttovalidatetheseprojectedoutcomes.
AppendixE. PerformanceLimitationsandBoundaryConditionsAcrossDifferentBuildingEnvironments
While the current study validates TLA-DETR within a controlled commercial office environment in Xi’an, we

The main contributions of this paper include 4 parts,
• Misinformation arising from superimposed central view
is found to be the main cause of non-Lambertian depth
estimation errors. Inconsistency caused by the central view
invalidates the algorithms based on Lambertian assumption.

EPI and an adaptive module enables ACO dynamically adjust
weights to different reflection coefficients.

papers.text:
WeatherDepth: Curriculum Contrastive Learning for Self-Supervised
Depth Estimation under Adverse Weather Conditions
Jiyuan Wang, Chunyu Lin†, Lang Nie, Shujun Huang, Yao Zhao, Xing Pan and Rui Ai
Abstract—Depth estimation models have shown promising
performance on clear scenes but fail to generalize to adverse
weather conditions due to illumination variations, weather
particles, etc. In this paper, we propose WeatherDepth, a self-
supervisedrobustdepthestimationmodelwithcurriculumcon-

Optics and Lasers in Engineering
journal homepage: www.elsevier.com/locate/optlaseng
Edge enhancement and feature modulation based network for light field
depth estimation
Xinjun Zhua,b,*, Ruiqin Tiana, Limei Songb,c, Hongyi Wanga,b, Qinghua Guod
aSchool of Artificial Intelligence, Tiangong University, Tianjin 300387, China
bTianjin Key Laboratory of Intelligent Control of Electrical Equipment, Tiangong University, Tianjin 300387, China
cSchool of Control Science and Engineering, Tiangong University, Tianjin 300387, China
dSchool of Electrical, Computer and Telecommunications Engineering, University of Wollongong, Wollongong, NSW 2522, Australia
A R T I C L E I N F O A B S T R A C T
Keywords: Estimating depth from light field images is a critical issue in light field applications. While learning-based
Light field methods have made significant strides in light field depth estimation, achieving high accuracy and speed
Depth estimation simultaneously remains a major challenge. This paper proposes a light field depth estimation network based on
Deep learning
edge enhancement and feature modulation, which significantly improves depth estimation results by empha-
Edge enhancement
sizing inter-view correlations while preserving image edge features. Specifically, to prioritize edge details, we
Feature modulation
introduce an Edge-Enhanced Cost Constructor (EECC) that integrates edge information with existing cost con-
structors to improve depth estimation performance in complex areas. Furthermore, most light field depth esti-
mation networks utilize only sub-aperture images (SAIs) without considering the inherent angular information in
macro-pixel image (MacPI). To address this limitation, we propose the MacPI-Guided Feature Modulation
(MGFM) module, which leverages angular information between different views in MacPI to modulate features at
each view. Experimental results show that our method not only performs excellently on synthetic datasets but
also demonstrates outstanding generalization on real-world datasets, achieving a better balance between accu-
racy and computation speed.
1. Introduction methods have undergone a long-term development and optimization,
possessing a relatively mature theoretical framework and algorithmic
Light field (LF) cameras can record 4D spatial and angle information process. They are capable of stable and reliable handling of disparity
of light rays in static scenes, endowing the LF images with advantages in estimation problems. Despite these advantages, traditional methods also
reconstructing complex scenes. By utilizing the rich spatial and angular face challenges, such as the lack of sufficient prior information which
information in LF images, it aids in depth estimation, super-resolution, may impact estimation accuracy. They also suffer from a large compu-
image segmentation, object detection, and other image processing tation amount. In recent years, deep learning has been introduced and
tasks [1]. LF depth estimation, as a fundamental prerequisite for sub- developed in the field of light field imaging, demonstrating significant
sequent applications, has become crucial in the fields such as computer potential to address these issues [7]. Furthermore, learning-based
vision. In addition, LF depth estimation, with its rich information, has methods can directly learn feature representations and disparity esti-
become a promising way for obtaining dense depth information, which mation from LF data through end-to-end learning. This approach avoids
complements monocular and binocular depth estimation. Therefore, the manual design of features and constraints required in traditional
accurately estimating depth information from LF images attracts more methods. End-to-end learning effectively utilizes data to automatically
and more attention. perform feature learning, thereby enhancing the accuracy and robust-
Currently, LF depth estimation methods include traditional methods ness of disparity estimation. Depth estimation based on deep learning
and learning-based methods [2,3], each with its own advantages. typically includes four steps: feature extraction, cost volume construc-
Traditional methods mainly include sub-aperture image stereo tion, cost aggregation, and depth regression. During the cost volume
matching-based [4], EPI-based [5] and refocus-based [6]. These construction process, most methods simply shift the sub-aperture image

papers.text:
Revolutionizing optical imaging: computational
imaging via deep learning
Xiyuan Luo,a,† Sen Wang,a,† Jinpeng Liu,a,b,† Xue Dong,a Piao He,a Qingyu Yang,a Xi Chen,a Feiyan Zhou,a
TongZhang,aShijieFeng,bPingliHan,a,cZhimingZhou,aMengXiang,a,cJiamingQian,bHaigangMa,bShunZhou,b
Linpeng Lu,b Chao Zuo,b,* Zihan Geng,d,* Yi Wei,e,* and Fei Liua,c,*
aSchoolofOptoelectronicEngineering,XidianUniversity,Xi’an,China
bSchoolofElectronicandOpticalEngineering,NanjingUniversityofScienceandTechnology,Nanjing,China
cXi’anKeyLaboratoryofComputationalImaging,Xi’an,China
dInstituteofDataandInformation,TsinghuaShenzhenInternationalGraduateSchool,TsinghuaUniversity,Shenzhen,China
eDepartmentofMechanicalEngineering,MassachusettsInstituteofTechnology,Cambridge,USA
Abstract. The current state of traditional optoelectronic imaging technology is constrained by the inherent
limitations of its hardware. These limitations pose significant challenges in acquiring higher-dimensional
informationandreconstructingaccurateimages,particularlyinapplicationssuchasscatteringimaging,super-
resolution, and complex scene reconstruction. However, the rapid development and widespread adoption of
deep learning are reshaping the field of optical imaging through computational imaging technology. Data-
driven computational imaging has ushered in a paradigm shift by leveraging the nonlinear expression
and feature learning capabilities of neural networks. This approach transcends the limitations of
conventional physical models, enabling the adaptive extraction of critical features directly from data. As a
result, computational imaging overcomes the traditional “what you see is what you get” paradigm, paving
the way for more compact optical system designs, broader information acquisition, and improved image
reconstruction accuracy. These advancements have significantly enhanced the interpretation of high-
dimensional light-field information and the processing of complex images. This review presents a
comprehensive analysis of the integration of deep learning and computational imaging, emphasizing its
transformative potential in three core areas: computational optical system design, high-dimensional
information interpretation, and image enhancement and processing. Additionally, this review addresses
the challenges and future directions of this cutting-edge technology, providing novel insights into
interdisciplinary imaging research.
Keywords:deep learning;computational imaging; opticalsystemdesign;high-dimensional information; imageprocessing.

(1) Target polarization characterization
Polarized 3D imaging. Since the 1970s, researchers in the
field have been investigating methodologies for three-dimen-
sional shape recovery using polarization information[357–361].
This led to the development of various polarization-based 3D
imaging techniques, collectively known as the shape-from-
polarization(SFP)method.TheSFPmethodutilizesthepolari-
zation information of reflected light to estimate the shape of
objects,effectivelyreducingthelossofsurfacetextureandmit-
igating the challenges posed by poor lighting conditions[362].
The SFP method offers several advantages, including high ac-
curacy, a long working distance, non-contact operation, and
robust anti-interference capabilities. Even in low-illumination
environments, the polarization characteristics of the reflected
light can still be captured, ensuring reliable shape estimation.
However, accurate target information can be compromised
bysurfacenormalambiguity,necessitatingtheuseofadditional
constraints or auxiliary information to resolve this issue[363,364].
Koshikawa[357] addressed this challenge by computing the sur-
face normal of the target through the analysis of the change
in the polarization state of circularly polarized light reflected
from the surface of an insulator. Smith[365] proposed a method

In this paper, we have presented an LF depth estimation
framework by taking into account the geometry structure of
EPI for LF. The relationship between the depth and slope
of the linear structure in the EPI is studied, inspired by
